blockingIssues.description: The prior let-binding hole is fixed, but `check_return` still accepts `return w.write("hello")` in void-returning handlers. Codegen emits `return w.Write([]byte("hello"))`, so `rgoc` still allows an unmodeled value-position `w.write(...)` use that only fails later in `go build` with `too many return values`.

rejectedObservations.observation: Clarify the `.factory/library/go-interop.md` contract for `w.write` to state whether the underlying Go `(int, error)` result is exposed in rgo, discarded only in statement position, or forbidden in every value-expression context.
